cover_caption: Autotune Progress: 276 Experiments, 29 Kept Improvements — validation BPB steadily decreasing as experiments progress
source_title: autoresearch: A framework for autonomous AI research agents

ai_summary.one_liner: Open-source framework for AI agents to autonomously iterate on ML training — 700 experiments in 2 days, 11% faster GPT-2
ai_summary.problem: Human researcher is the bottleneck in the iterative ML experiment loop — can only test one hypothesis at a time
ai_summary.solution: autoresearch: 3-file framework (prepare.py, train.py, program.md) with fixed 5-min time budget and single val_bpb metric, letting AI agents autonomously modify and evaluate
ai_summary.key_result: GPT-2 training time 2.02h → 1.80h (11% reduction) in ~700 autonomous experiments over 2 days; found real bugs (QKnorm, missing regularization) that transferred to larger models
limitations: Hyperparameter tuning level, not novel research

figures.caption: autoresearch's initial run log. Over 12 hours, the AI agent tried 110 changes, reducing the d12 model's validation loss from 0.862415 to 0.858039. DISCARDED/KEPT decisions are made automatically.
figures.caption_ko: autoresearch의 첫 실행 로그. 12시간 동안 AI 에이전트가 110개의 변경을 시도하며 d12 모델의 validation loss를 0.862415에서 0.858039로 낮추었다. DISCARDED/KEPT 판정이 자동으로 이루어진다.

figures.caption: Autotune Progress: 276 Experiments, 29 Kept Improvements. The agent autonomously runs experiments, steadily reducing validation BPB. Gray dots are discarded experiments; green dots are kept improvements.
figures.caption_ko: Autotune Progress: 276 Experiments, 29 Kept Improvements. 에이전트가 자율적으로 실험을 수행하며 validation BPB를 지속적으로 낮추는 과정. 회색 점은 discard된 실험, 녹색 점은 keep된 개선이다.
